content: Curiosity-driven autonomous ICM agents can use a curiosity-weighted memory compression and summarization framework to retain only high-value unexpected experiences in long-term memory, rather than compressing all captured experiences uniformly. This allows agents to prioritize retaining rare, information-dense exploratory experiences that drive future exploration, cutting long-term memory storage costs while boosting sustained curiosity-driven learning.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
